summary: DHH reflects on advice from his 12th grade Danish teacher about the importance of rewriting. He admits he rarely followed the rewriting advice during school, but finds himself compulsively editing and refining his early blog posts. The short post is a lighthearted observation about how blogging has unexpectedly made him embrace revision.
key_points: His Danish teacher emphasized writing things down and then rewriting them as essential practice
As a student, DHH only followed the first half — writing but not revising
Blogging triggered an unexpected embrace of careful revision and sentence-level editing
money_quotes: If you got something on your mind, write it down. If you've written something down, rewrite it.
The papers I turned in usually only complied with the first part of his teaching.
I can't seem to get enough of the second part. Constantly evaluating each sentence. Changing subtle things.
key_insight: Publishing to an audience — even a blog audience — creates a natural incentive to revise and refine that schoolwork never did.
